mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.02 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts reflects broad-based consumer demand strength, which drives corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when either series declines, or the interaction term's marginal predictive power is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity utilization, which improves corporate profit margins and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal reverses direction or becomes statistically flat during supply chain normalization phases, or 20-day forward returns are negative in > 55% of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent monthly growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects sustained economic expansion, supporting consumer spending and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns, with mean forward returns remaining negative or flat across all expansion periods, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 310.0, persistent inflationary pressures force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, increasing borrowing costs and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in > 60% of periods where CPIAUCSL remains above 310.0, or the threshold effect disappears in out-of-sample walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent acceleration in real GDP and retail sales confirms robust aggregate demand, which translates into higher corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal loses predictive accuracy when GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.0% but retail sales stagnate, or the interaction effect yields a negative or null Rank IC in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal a contracting manufacturing sector, which reduces economic output and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative 5-day returns during recessionary downturns, or forward returns are positive in > 50% of triggered instances, invalidating the contractionary mechanism.
